stations.name: Morningside railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Closest station for southern Bulimba streets. Northern Bulimba is closer to ferry than train.

ferry.service: CityCat and CityFerry on the Brisbane River
ferry.note: Direct CityCat service to the CBD, South Bank, and UQ. The Bulimba terminal at the end of Oxford Street is one of the more practical river-to-CBD commutes in inner Brisbane.

buses.note: Bus routes serve Bulimba via Oxford Street, Riding Road, and Hawthorne Road. Routes and frequencies change with Translink service updates.

driving.motorway_access: Story Bridge for CBD travel. Gateway Motorway access via Hawthorne Road and the Cannon Hill interchange.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 8 to 12 minutes to Brisbane CBD off-peak via the Story Bridge.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_peak_note: Peak commute typically extends to 18 to 25 minutes.

connections: Brisbane Riverwalk and the Hawthorne riverside path

primary_state.name: Bulimba State School
primary_state.note: One of the most sought-after primary state school catchments in inner east Brisbane. The catchment boundary materially affects buyer interest on a street-by-street basis.

secondary_state.name: Balmoral State High School
secondary_state.note: Common secondary catchment for many Bulimba streets. Verify for any specific address.

lifestyle.shops_dining_note: Oxford Street is the inner-east village strip that sets the benchmark. Cafes, restaurants, bars, retail, and the heritage Bulimba cinema run end-to-end. Daily groceries at Bulimba Plaza. The Hawthorne Garage precinct adds another cluster of casual dining and cafes a short walk away.
lifestyle.river_access: Brisbane River frontage and ferry access at the end of Oxford Street.

pockets.name: Riverside streets
pockets.character: Streets within walking distance of the river and Oxford Street. Heritage queenslanders, premium character stock, and the most contested family-home market in the suburb.

pockets.name: Oxford Street precinct
pockets.character: Mix of character homes, contemporary builds, and townhouse stock close to the shopping strip. Higher density than the riverside streets.

pockets.name: Hawthorne-adjacent
pockets.character: Streets bordering Hawthorne with similar character and a slightly different price profile depending on which side of the boundary the address sits.

buyer_profile.note: Families targeting the Bulimba State School catchment and the Oxford Street lifestyle. This is the largest buyer segment in the suburb and the one campaigns are typically built around.

buyer_profile.type: Upsizers from Hawthorne, Morningside, Norman Park
buyer_profile.note: Buyers moving up from neighbouring suburbs who want the river access and the village amenity Bulimba offers.
Sydney and Melbourne relocators drawn by the inner-east lifestyle and the relative value compared to comparable Sydney or Melbourne suburbs.

buyer_profile.type: Downsizers staying in the inner east
buyer_profile.note: Long-term local owners selling family homes and buying smaller character homes or townhouses to stay in the area.

verify_flags: Exact Bulimba State School catchment boundary for any specific address
Secondary school catchment for any specific street
